title: Market Discovery: ZOLLERN Steel Profiles — New Application Markets

sections.title: Discovery Methodology
sections.content:
Three parallel discovery tracks were used to identify candidate markets:

| Track | Method | Logic | Markets Found |
|-------|--------|-------|---------------|
| Track A | Machining intensity analysis | Industries spending heavily on machining bar stock into 2D profiles are prime NNS targets | 8 |
| Track B | Application analogy | Current applications (elevator rails, linear guides) imply adjacent markets with similar geometry needs | 6 |
| Track C | Barrier-protected niches | Markets where certification/qualification barriers create durable competitive positions once entered | 4 |

Total unique candidates after deduplication: 14. After constraint filtering (C1: 2D only, C6: steel only), 11 survived. After minimum volume filter (>200 t/year SAM), 8 survived. Final ranking produced top 3.

sections.title: Constraint Pre-Filter Results
sections.content:
| Candidate | C1 (2D) | C6 (Steel) | Min Volume | Status |
|-----------|---------|-----------|-----------|--------|
| Elevator guide rails | PASS | PASS | PASS (>5,000 t) | QUALIFIED |
| Hydraulic cylinder tubes | PASS | PASS | PASS (>3,000 t) | QUALIFIED |
| Agricultural equipment | PASS | PASS | PASS (>2,000 t) | QUALIFIED |
| Linear motion guides | PASS | PASS | PASS (~800 t) | QUALIFIED |
| Rack-and-pinion rails | PASS | PASS | PASS (~600 t) | QUALIFIED |
| Medical instrument blanks | PASS | PASS | FAIL (<50 t) | ELIMINATED |
| Architectural facade mullions | PASS | FAIL (aluminum) | - | ELIMINATED |
| Aerospace wing spars | FAIL (tapered) | FAIL (titanium) | - | ELIMINATED |

sections.title: Ranked Market List
sections.content:
### Rank #1: Elevator Guide Rails (NAICS 333921)

**Score: 87/100**

The elevator guide rail market is the highest-priority target due to: (a) large and growing global volume, (b) ZOLLERN's existing presence proving technical feasibility, (c) high certification barriers creating defensible positions, and (d) strong NNS advantage over machined alternatives. [SRC: web_search]

| Factor | Score | Rationale |
|--------|-------|-----------|
| Volume absorption | 9/10 | Global T-rail market ~250,000 t/year; DACH SAM ~12,000 t/year |
| NNS advantage | 9/10 | 35% material savings vs machined bar; grain flow improves rail head wear |
| Certification barrier | 8/10 | EN 81-1/EN 81-20 + EN 81-50 type testing required |
| Competitive vulnerability | 7/10 | Market dominated by 4 players; room for new entrant with cost advantage |
| Feature fit | 9/10 | T-profile geometry, induction hardening, straightness — all within envelope |
| Margin potential | 8/10 | EUR 1,200-1,800/t vs commodity steel at EUR 600-800/t |
| Existing capability | 10/10 | ZOLLERN already produces T89/B guide rails for select customers |
| Strategic alignment | 9/10 | Absorbs idle hot rolling + induction hardening capacity |

### Rank #2: Hydraulic Cylinder Tubes (NAICS 332912)

**Score: 74/100**

Precision round tubes for hydraulic cylinders — cold drawn seamless tubes with tight ID tolerance and honed surface finish. Large global market but intense price competition from Asian producers. [SRC: web_search]

| Factor | Score | Rationale |
|--------|-------|-----------|
| Volume absorption | 8/10 | European market ~80,000 t/year |
| NNS advantage | 6/10 | Moderate — tubes already near-net but ZOLLERN could offer pre-honed |
| Certification barrier | 5/10 | ISO 3320 dimensional, but no major type testing |
| Competitive vulnerability | 5/10 | Crowded: Salzgitter, Benteler, Mannesmann, Asian imports |
| Feature fit | 7/10 | Round profiles, tight tolerance — within cold drawing envelope |
| Margin potential | 5/10 | Commoditized; EUR 900-1,200/t |
| Existing capability | 7/10 | Round cold drawing exists; would need tube capability (hollow) |
| Strategic alignment | 7/10 | Absorbs cold drawing capacity |

### Rank #3: Agricultural Equipment Wear Parts (NAICS 333111)

**Score: 69/100**

Wear-resistant steel profiles for agricultural machinery: plow points, cultivator tines, disc harrow edges. Requires hardened carbon steel profiles with specific cross-sections. Seasonal demand pattern. [SRC: web_search]

| Factor | Score | Rationale |
|--------|-------|-----------|
| Volume absorption | 7/10 | European replacement parts market ~30,000 t/year |
| NNS advantage | 7/10 | Currently machined from flat bar; NNS profile saves 25-40% |
| Certification barrier | 3/10 | Low — no formal certification, just OEM qualification |
| Competitive vulnerability | 6/10 | Fragmented market, many small forges and stampers |
| Feature fit | 7/10 | Custom asymmetric profiles with hardening — good match |
| Margin potential | 6/10 | EUR 1,000-1,400/t |
| Existing capability | 6/10 | Hot rolling + hardening exists; new die designs needed |
| Strategic alignment | 6/10 | Seasonal demand (Q1-Q2 peak) complements other markets |

sections.title: Discovery Sources
sections.content:
| Source Type | Count | Examples |
|------------|-------|---------|
| Industry databases | 3 | VDMA statistics, Eurostat PRODCOM, UN Comtrade |
| Trade publications | 5 | Stahl und Eisen, Elevator World, Fluid Power Journal |
| Patent databases | 2 | EPO, USPTO (guide rail profile patents) |
| Company websites | 12 | Montanstahl, Savera, Bosch Rexroth, John Deere suppliers |
| Expert interviews | 0 | Not conducted in discovery phase |
